title: brassica saxatilis subsp. blancoana
intro: Brassica saxatilis subsp. blancoana, also known as wild cabbage or sea kale, is a plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is a perennial crop with medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and stems, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations, offering benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Brassica saxatilis subsp. blancoana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and stems can also be consumed raw or cooked, making it a versatile and valuable medicinal herb.
